articles.headline: Iran war heralds era of AI-powered bombing quicker than ‘speed of thought’
articles.description: Speed and scale of US military’s AI war planning raises fears human decision-making may be sidelinedThe use of AI tools to enable attacks on Iran heralds a new era of bombing quicker than “the speed of thought”, experts have said, amid fears human ­decision-makers could be sidelined.Anthropic’s AI model, Claude, was reportedly used by the US military in the barrage of strikes as the technology “shortens the kill chain” – meaning the process of target identification through to legal approval and strike launch. Continue reading...

articles.headline: Alibaba just released Qwen 3.5 Small models: a family of 0.8B to 9B parameters built for on-device applications
articles.description: Alibaba’s Qwen team has released the Qwen3.5 Small Model Series, a collection of Large Language Models (LLMs) ranging from 0.8B to 9B parameters. While the industry trend has historically favored increasing parameter counts to achieve ‘frontier’ performance, this release focuses on ‘More Intelligence, Less Compute.‘ These models represent a shift toward deploying capable AI on […] The post Alibaba just released Qwen 3.5 Small models: a family of 0.8B to 9B parameters built for on-device applications appeared first on MarkTechPost.

articles.headline: Nvidia’s spending $4 billion on photonics to stay ahead of the curve in AI
articles.description: Nvidia announced on Monday that it's investing $2 billion each into Lumentum and Coherent, which are both developing photonics technology for data centers, like optical transceivers, circuit switches, and lasers, which are used to move data at high speeds over long distances. Their tech could improve energy efficiency, data transfer speeds, and bandwidth in future […]

articles.headline: I’m on the Meta Oversight Board. We need AI protections now | Suzanne Nossel
articles.description: AI is transforming our world. Accepting independent oversight is the least companies can do to protect our rightsThe speed with which AI is transforming our lives is head-spinning. Unlike previous technological revolutions – radio, nuclear fission or the internet – governments are not leading the way. We know that AI can be dangerous; chatbots advise teens on suicide and may soon be capable of instructing on how to create biological weapons. Yet there is no equivalent to the Federal Drug Administration, testing new models for safety before public release. Unlike in the nuclear industry, companies often don’t have to disclose dangerous breaches or accidents. The tech industry’s lobbying muscle, Washington’s paralyzing polarization, and the sheer complexity of such a potent, fast-moving technology have kept federal regulation at bay. European officials are facing pushback against rules that some claim hobble the continent’s competitiveness. Although several US states are piloting AI laws, they operate in a tentative patchwork and Donald Trump has attempted to render them invalid.Heads of AI platforms like OpenAI’s ChatGPT and Google’s Gemini say they care about safety. But owning the future of AI means pouring billions into models that not even their creators fully understand, and making choices like adding ads – and the capabilities that the Pentagon is now seeking from Anthropic – that raise risk. Anthropic, which styles itself as the most conscientious frontier AI company, says its model is trained to “imagine how a thoughtful senior Anthropic employee” would weigh helpfulness against possible harm. The directive echoes criticisms levied years ago over Silicon Valley companies that shaped the lives of users worldwide from insular boardrooms. Consumers don’t believe they are in good hands. Fully 77% of Americans surveyed last year think AI could pose a threat to humanity. Continue reading...

software_engineering.headline: US Military Uses Anthropic's Claude AI for Faster Strike Planning
software_engineering.what_changed: The US military reportedly used Anthropic's Claude AI model to accelerate target identification, legal approval, and strike launches against Iran.
software_engineering.why_care: This demonstrates how AI is being integrated into critical, real-time decision-making systems, highlighting the need for robust, auditable, and secure AI deployments.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Investigate how AI models are being integrated into your organization's critical systems and assess their ethical and security implications.

software_engineering.headline: Qualcomm CEO Predicts Robotics Will Be a Major Opportunity Within 2 Years
software_engineering.what_changed: Qualcomm's CEO, Cristiano Amon, stated that robotics will become a larger market opportunity for the company within two years.
software_engineering.why_care: This indicates a growing market for embedded systems and specialized processors, creating new opportunities for you to develop software for robotic applications.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Explore Qualcomm's Dragonwing processor and other robotics platforms to understand the development landscape for future robotic systems.

software_engineering.headline: Alibaba Releases Qwen 3.5 Small Models for On-Device AI Applications
software_engineering.what_changed: Alibaba's Qwen team released the Qwen 3.5 Small Model Series, a collection of Large Language Models ranging from 0.8B to 9B parameters designed for on-device deployment.
software_engineering.why_care: These smaller, efficient models enable you to integrate powerful AI capabilities directly into edge devices and mobile applications, reducing reliance on cloud infrastructure.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Experiment with the Qwen 3.5 Small models to evaluate their performance and suitability for your on-device or edge computing projects.

software_engineering.headline: Nvidia Invests $4 Billion in Photonics for AI Data Centers
software_engineering.what_changed: Nvidia is investing $2 billion each into Lumentum and Coherent, totaling $4 billion, to advance photonics technology for data centers.
software_engineering.why_care: This investment aims to improve data transfer speeds, bandwidth, and energy efficiency in data centers, which will directly impact the performance and cost of your cloud-based AI workloads.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Monitor developments in photonics and optical networking, as these advancements will influence future data center architectures and your application deployment strategies.

software_engineering.headline: Meta Oversight Board Member Calls for Urgent AI Protections
software_engineering.what_changed: Suzanne Nossel, a member of the Meta Oversight Board, emphasized the urgent need for independent oversight and regulation of AI to protect user rights and prevent harm.
software_engineering.why_care: The lack of clear AI regulations means you must proactively consider ethical AI development, data privacy, and security in your projects to mitigate risks and ensure responsible innovation.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Review your current AI development practices to ensure they incorporate ethical guidelines, robust testing, and transparency measures.

hr.what_to_do: Review your organization's current AI ethics guidelines and discuss potential scenarios where AI might influence sensitive decisions, ensuring human review remains paramount.

hr.headline: Qualcomm CEO Predicts Robotics Will Be a Major Opportunity Within 2 Years
hr.what_changed: Qualcomm's CEO believes robotics will become a larger market opportunity than their current core business within two years, following the launch of their Dragonwing processor for robots.
hr.why_care: This signals a rapid expansion in the robotics industry, which could impact future workforce needs, automation of tasks, and the types of skills your organization will require.
hr.what_to_do: Begin researching how robotics might integrate into your industry or specific job functions and identify potential future skill gaps in your workforce.

hr.headline: Alibaba Releases Qwen 3.5 Small AI Models for On-Device Use
hr.what_changed: Alibaba's Qwen team released a new series of smaller Large Language Models, Qwen 3.5 Small, designed for efficient deployment on individual devices rather than large data centers.
hr.why_care: This shift towards 'More Intelligence, Less Compute' means AI tools could become more accessible and integrated into everyday devices and software your employees use, potentially streamlining workflows.
hr.what_to_do: Explore how smaller, on-device AI models could be used to enhance productivity tools or internal applications within your HR department, such as for quick data analysis or content generation.

hr.headline: Nvidia Invests $4 Billion in Photonics for AI Data Centers
hr.what_changed: Nvidia is investing $4 billion into Lumentum and Coherent, companies developing photonics technology to improve data transfer speeds and energy efficiency in future AI data centers.
hr.why_care: This investment signals a continued rapid advancement in AI infrastructure, meaning the capabilities and accessibility of AI tools you might use will likely increase significantly.
hr.what_to_do: Stay informed about the general trends in AI infrastructure development to anticipate future AI tool capabilities that could benefit your HR operations.

hr.headline: Meta Oversight Board Member Calls for Urgent AI Protections
hr.what_changed: Suzanne Nossel, a Meta Oversight Board member, emphasized the urgent need for independent oversight and regulation of AI to protect rights, citing concerns about safety and the lack of government leadership.
hr.why_care: The call for AI protections highlights the critical importance of establishing clear ethical guidelines and governance for AI tools you implement, ensuring fairness and mitigating risks to employees.
hr.what_to_do: Initiate a discussion with your legal and IT teams to develop or refine your organization's internal policies for responsible AI use, focusing on data privacy, bias, and transparency.
